[REASONING CONTEXT]
Amplify: discount_rate_auditing; temporal_horizon_normalization; present_bias_flagging
Suppress: single_discount_rate_assumption; time_consistent_preference_default
Cognitive Style: intertemporal_simulation — track each month sequentially, no shortcuts
[END REASONING CONTEXT]

Working through this month by month. The order each month is: **interest → check fee → add fee → subtract payment**.

| Month | Start Balance | After 2% Interest | Late Fee | After Fee | After $150 Payment |
|-------|--------------|-------------------|----------|-----------|-------------------|
| 1 | $5,000.00 | $5,100.00 | $75 (>5k) | $5,175.00 | **$5,025.00** |
| 2 | $5,025.00 | $5,125.50 | $75 | $5,200.50 | **$5,050.50** |
| 3 | $5,050.50 | $5,151.51 | $75 | $5,226.51 | **$5,076.51** |
| 4 | $5,076.51 | $5,178.04 | $75 | $5,253.04 | **$5,103.04** |
| 5 | $5,103.04 | $5,205.10 | $75 | $5,280.10 | **$5,130.10** |
| 6 | $5,130.10 | $5,232.70 | $75 | $5,307.70 | **$5,157.70** |

Every month the post-interest balance exceeds $5,000, so the $75 fee applies all 6 months. The $150 payment never overcomes the combined interest + fee (~$175/month), so the balance grows.

**After 6 months, the loan balance is $5,158.**
